Change 1: Replace:
  # Combine all clauses into one fluent sentence
      # Start with the first clause as the main statement
      sentence = clauses[0]
      for idx, cl in enumerate(clauses[1:], start=1):
          # Use "which" for the first added clause, then "and" for the rest

with:
  # Combine all clauses into one fluent sentence, avoiding repetition of the subject
      first_subject = triples[0].subject
      sentence = clauses[0]
      for idx, cl in enumerate(clauses[1:], start=1):
          # Choose appropriate connector
          connector = "which" if idx == 1 else "and"
          # Strip repeated subject or possessive form at the start of the clause
          cl_mod = cl
          subj_space = f"{first_subject} "
          subj_poss = f"{first_subject}'s "
          if cl_mod.lower().startswith(subj_space.lower()):
              cl_mod = cl_mod[len(subj_space):]
          elif cl_mod.lower().startswith(subj_poss.lower()):
              cl_mod = cl_mod[len(subj_poss):]
          # Lower‑case the first character of the remaining clause for smooth flow
          if cl_mod:
              cl_mod = cl_mod[0].lower() + cl_mod[1:]
          sentence += f", {connector} {cl_mod}"
      sentence += "."
